title: Dissociation of Human Neuroblastoma Tumors for Single-Cell RNA-seq
author: Anand Patel
license_note: Confirmed via CrossRef DOI metadata: dx.doi.org/10.17504/protocols.io.98ah9se
materials: Prepare reagents (1 set for each sample):

steps.title: Add 5 mL of Earle's Balanced...
steps.instruction: Add 5 mL of Earle's Balanced Salt Solution (vial #1) to papain (vial #2). Allow papain to dissolve for 15 min at 37° C. 37 °C 00:15:00

steps.title: Add 500 µL of Earle's Balanced...
steps.instruction: Add 500 µL of Earle's Balanced Salt Solution (vial #1) to DNase I (vial #3). Mix using micropipette. Room temperature

steps.title: Add 250 µL from DNase I...
steps.instruction: Add 250 µL from DNase I solution (vial #3) to papain solution (vial #2). Room temperature

steps.title: Obtain tumor tissue
steps.instruction: Obtain tumor tissue. Use 100-500 mg of tissue per papain vial. Use a disposable scalpel to mince the sample into small pieces (approximately 1 mm fragments). Room temperature, Process tissue quickly to avoid viability loss (mincing should take less than 5 min) Note: We typically receive tissue on ice from the repository. Tissue is processed immediately upon receipt. Once dissociation has started, we do not recommend putting tissue back onto ice (temperature fluctuations lead to tissue degradation). Note: We typically mince tissue on a 60 mm sterile tissue-culture treated plate.

steps.title: Move minced tissue into a sterile...
steps.instruction: Move minced tissue into a sterile 15 mL Falcon conical tube, and transfer papain-DNase mixture from vial #2 to the conical tube. Incubate conical tube in water bath at 37° C for 15 min. 37 °C, Water bath 00:15:00

steps.title: Triturate tissue by pipetting up and...
steps.instruction: Triturate tissue by pipetting up and down 10-15 times using a 10 mL sterile serologic pipette. Room temperature

steps.title: Continue dissociation by incubating tube in...
steps.instruction: Continue dissociation by incubating tube in the water bath for another 15 min. 37 °C, Water bath 00:15:00

steps.title: Repeat trituration by pipetting up and...
steps.instruction: Repeat trituration by pipetting up and down 10-15 times using a 10 mL sterile serologic pipette. Room temperature Note: The cell suspension should look cloudy with chunks of debris.

steps.title: Filter cell suspension through a 40...
steps.instruction: Filter cell suspension through a 40 µm cell strainer into a 50 mL Falcon conical tube. Room temperature Note: Debris will be captured on the cell strainer, while the filtrate should appear cloudy.

steps.title: Rinse filter with 5 mL Earle's...
steps.instruction: Rinse filter with 5 mL Earle's Balanced Salt Solution (vial #1).

steps.title: Centrifuge cell suspension at 500 g...
steps.instruction: Centrifuge cell suspension at 500 g for 5 min at room temperature. 500 x g, °C, 5 minutes Room temperature

steps.title: During centrifugation, prepare the two 15...
steps.instruction: During centrifugation, prepare the two 15 mL Falcon conical tubes containing:

steps.title: After centrifugation (step #9), remove supernatant...
steps.instruction: After centrifugation (step #9), remove supernatant carefully without disturbing the pellet. Tip: We typically use suction to aspirate the first 9 mL of supernatant, followed by a micropipette to remove the last 0.5-1 mL of supernatant (to avoid suctioning the pellet). If the pellet is very small or difficult to see, we recommend transferring the top 9.5 mL of supernatant to a tube labeled 'supernatant 1' as a backup. Room temperature

steps.title: Re-suspend pellet with 3 mL resuspension buffer (from step #10
steps.instruction: Re-suspend pellet with 3 mL resuspension buffer (from step #10.1), and gently mix using a 5 mL serologic pipette. Room temperature

steps.title: Density gradient
steps.instruction: Density gradient: 5 mL, Albumin-ovomucoid inhibitor solution (vial #4) Room temperature

steps.title: Carefully layer resuspended cells over density gradient (from step #10
steps.instruction: Carefully layer resuspended cells over density gradient (from step #10.2). Room temperature

steps.title: Centrifuge at 100 g for 6 min at room temperature
steps.instruction: Centrifuge at 100 g for 6 min at room temperature. 100 x g, °C, 6 minutes Room temperature

steps.title: Carefully remove supernatant without disturbing the pellet
steps.instruction: Carefully remove supernatant without disturbing the pellet. Tip: We typically use suction to aspirate the first 7 mL of supernatant, followed by a micropipette to remove the last 0.5-1 mL of supernatant (to avoid suctioning the pellet). If the pellet is very small or difficult to see, we recommend transferring the top 7.5 mL of supernatant to a tube labeled 'supernatant 2' as a backup.

steps.title: Mix 10 µL of single-cell suspension...
steps.instruction: Mix 10 µL of single-cell suspension with 10 µL of Trypan blue solution, and load 10 µL onto hemacytometer. Room temperature

steps.title: Count and report the number of viable single cells. Tip
steps.instruction: Count and report the number of viable single cells. Tip: We recommend proceeding only with samples that have >80% viability. Low viability samples will have higher cell clumping, and ambient RNA will complicate downstream single-cell RNA-sequencing analysis. Room temperature

steps.title: If necessary, dilute cells prior to...
steps.instruction: If necessary, dilute cells prior to loading onto 10x Genomics 3' single cell RNA-sequencing workflow. Tip: We aim to load 10,000 cells per channel. We recommend trying to dilute cells to a concentration 1000 cells/µL.

steps.title: Keep diluted cell suspension on ice,...
steps.instruction: Keep diluted cell suspension on ice, and proceed immediately to 10x Genomics single-cell RNA-sequencing workflow. On ice, Proceed immediately to 10x Genomics RNA-seq workflow

steps.title: Re-suspend pellet in 10 mL Earle's...
steps.instruction: Re-suspend pellet in 10 mL Earle's Balanced Salt Solution and filter through a 40 µm cell strainer into a sterile 50 mL Falcon conical tube. Room temperature
